 {"id":"bd-1ei","title":"demo_showcase spec: content pack (sample code, logs, links, unicode strings, metrics)","description":"# demo_showcase Spec — Content Pack

## Purpose

The demo needs a high-quality “content pack” so it looks like a real application and also proves correctness:

- realistic code to edit (highlighting + editing)
- logs that look like real systems output
- deterministic metrics for charts
- Unicode strings that prove grapheme + width correctness
- hyperlink URLs to prove OSC 8 integration

This bead defines the canonical content sets so every other bead can reference them without re-inventing strings.

## Content Sets

### 1) Editor Sample Content (highlighting + editing)

Primary buffer: a small Rust module that contains:

- `struct`/`enum` + `impl`
- lifetimes + generics
- doc comments + line comments
- `match`, `if let`, `Result`/`?`
- strings with escapes + raw strings
- a few `TODO:` style comments (to look realistic)

Secondary buffer (optional): Markdown with fenced Rust code blocks to prove tokenizer switching (if supported by the built-in registries).

**Important constraint:** keep the editor sample mostly to single-codepoint characters. Avoid multi-codepoint graphemes (ZWJ emoji, combining sequences) in the editor text itself, because `EditorView::render_to` does not use the grapheme pool path.

### 2) Logs Content (event stream)

The logs panel should show an event stream with:

- timestamps
- `INFO/WARN/ERROR` levels
- subsystem tags (`renderer`, `input`, `preview`, `tour`, etc.)
- a few “actionable” entries that are linkified via OSC 8

Example (conceptual):

- `[22:05:12] INFO  renderer: diff=124 cells, bytes=3.1KB`
- `[22:05:13] WARN  input: focus lost (paused)`
- `[22:05:14] ERROR preview: simulated failure (press R to retry)`

### 3) Metrics Content (charts + animation)

Metrics must be deterministic (derived from time + fixed parameters):

- FPS + frame time
- “CPU usage” synthetic waveform
- “memory bytes” synthetic counter
- a periodic “pulse” value for glow animations

No randomness (so tour mode is reproducible and tests can assert on values).

### 4) Unicode / Width Prove‑Out Strings (pool-rendered)

These strings must be rendered using the grapheme pool path (`buffer_with_pool` + `draw_text_with_pool` / `render_to_with_pool`) so correctness is visible.

Include at least:

- CJK wide chars: `漢字かなカナ`
- single-codepoint emoji: `🎉👍😀`
- multi-codepoint ZWJ emoji: `👨‍👩‍👧` / `👩‍💻` / `🧑‍🚀`
- combining marks: `a\u{0301}` (á) and `n\u{0303}` (ñ)

Also include a fixed “width ruler” line under each string so a viewer can see alignment and width decisions.

### 5) Hyperlink URLs (OSC 8)

Use stable URLs that are obviously legitimate:

- `https://github.com/anomalyco/opentui`
- `https://github.com/anomalyco/opentui/tree/main/src`
- `https://unicode.org/reports/tr11/`

The exact URLs are less important than:

- they’re real
- they’re visually recognizable
- at least one is rendered in a log row and in a “docs”/help context

## Why This Matters

A demo’s credibility lives or dies on its content. If the strings are artificial or the logs look fake, the viewer won’t trust the engine.","acceptance_criteria":"Checklist:
- [ ] Provide realistic sample Rust code (highlights look great)
- [ ] Provide deterministic synthetic log stream
- [ ] Provide deterministic metric generators for charts
- [ ] Provide unicode strings that prove width + grapheme pool
- [ ] Provide a few real URLs for OSC 8 hyperlink demo","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:05.501955219Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:24:48.502816677Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:24:48.502719196Z","close_reason":"Completed: added content module with EDITOR_SAMPLE_RUST, EDITOR_SAMPLE_MARKDOWN, LogEntry/LOG_ENTRIES, deterministic Metrics::compute(), unicode submodule (CJK, emoji, combining marks), and links submodule (OSC 8 URLs)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["content","demo_showcase","design"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ei","depends_on_id":"bd-2jc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:05.532952574Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ei","depends_on_id":"bd-2mu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:00.588621535Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1fu","title":"demo_showcase UI: sidebar nav (scrollable list + scissor + mouse selection)","description":"# demo_showcase UI — Sidebar Navigation

## Purpose

Implement a scrollable sidebar that proves:

- nested scissor clipping
- scroll offsets
- mouse wheel scrolling
- click hit testing per row

…and that drives the app’s **Section** navigation coherently.

## Content

Sidebar items (example):

- Overview
- Editor
- Preview
- Logs
- Unicode
- Performance
- Settings

## Behavior

- Up/Down selects item (when focused)
- Mouse wheel scrolls list (when hovered)
- Click selects item and focuses sidebar

Navigation semantics:

- Selecting an item updates `App.section` (see state machine bead).
- Navigating to a section also sets a sensible default focus:
  - Editor -> focus editor
  - Preview/Unicode/Performance -> focus preview
  - Logs -> focus logs

In compact layout, `section` determines which panel is shown full-screen.

## Rendering

- Panel border (focused highlights)
- Header “NAV”
- Scrollbar indicator
- Selected row uses selection background (alpha overlay)

## HitGrid

- Register a hit area per visible row.
- ID encoding scheme: `HIT_SIDEBAR_BASE + row_index`.","acceptance_criteria":"Checklist:
- [ ] Sidebar list renders and scrolls inside clip rect
- [ ] Selected row is styled clearly
- [ ] Mouse wheel scroll works
- [ ] Click selects row via HitGrid
- [ ] Keyboard navigation works when focused","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"RusticSnow","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:19.214890317Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T01:27:45.304965528Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T01:27:45.304831178Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1fu","depends_on_id":"bd-1pd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:31:51.731520632Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fu","depends_on_id":"bd-35g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:31:52.274517829Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fu","depends_on_id":"bd-3ii","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:31:52.841934125Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fu","depends_on_id":"bd-c75","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:19.248313963Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fu","depends_on_id":"bd-hc0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:21.185238691Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fu","depends_on_id":"bd-po1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:25.354690846Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1gff","title":"Subtask: PTY CI Infrastructure","description":"# PTY CI Infrastructure

## Scope
Set up CI to run PTY tests reliably.

## Requirements

### Runner Configuration
- Ubuntu runner (PTY support)
- Allocate PTY before tests
- Set TERM environment variable
- Install expect if needed

### Job Configuration
\`\`\`yaml
pty-tests:
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  timeout-minutes: 15
  env:
    TERM: xterm-256color
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    
    - name: Setup Rust
      uses: dtolnay/rust-action@stable
      
    - name: Build
      run: cargo build --release --bin demo_showcase
      
    - name: Run PTY tests
      run: |
        # Allocate PTY for test process
        script -q -c "cargo test --test pty_e2e -- --ignored --nocapture" /dev/null
        
    - name: Upload logs
      if: always()
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v3
      with:
        name: pty-logs
        path: target/pty-test-logs/
\`\`\`

### Artifact Capture
- PTY session logs
- Test output
- Timing information
- Failure screenshots (if possible)

### Retry Configuration
- Allow 1 retry for flaky tests
- Separate job for PTY tests (doesn't block main CI)

## Files to Create
- .github/workflows/pty-tests.yml

## Acceptance Criteria
- PTY tests run in CI
- Artifacts captured
- Flakiness < 5%
- Clear failure reporting","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:48:16.423544704Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:49:31.080879681Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1gff","depends_on_id":"bd-1sbn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:49:31.080844655Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1gff","depends_on_id":"bd-3le2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:48:16.435934162Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1ghx","title":"Subtask: Golden File Comparison Utilities","description":"# Golden File Comparison Utilities

## Scope
Create utilities for comparing actual output to golden files.

## Components

### GoldenFile Loader
\`\`\`rust
struct GoldenFile {
    name: String,
    terminal_size: (u32, u32),
    capabilities: TermCaps,
    content: Vec<u8>,
}

impl GoldenFile {
    fn load(path: &Path) -> Result<Self>;
    fn save(path: &Path, content: &[u8]) -> Result<()>;
}
\`\`\`

### Comparison Engine
\`\`\`rust
enum DiffResult {
    Match,
    Mismatch { 
        expected: Vec<u8>,
        actual: Vec<u8>,
        diff: Vec<DiffLine>,
    },
}

fn compare_golden(golden: &GoldenFile, actual: &[u8]) -> DiffResult;
\`\`\`

### Diff Visualization
\`\`\`rust
fn render_diff_html(diff: &DiffResult) -> String;
fn render_diff_terminal(diff: &DiffResult) -> String;
\`\`\`

### Update Mode
\`\`\`rust
// GOLDEN_UPDATE=1 cargo test -> updates golden files
fn maybe_update_golden(golden: &GoldenFile, actual: &[u8]) -> bool;
\`\`\`

## Integration
\`\`\`rust
#[test]
fn test_empty_buffer() {
    let actual = render_empty_buffer();
    assert_golden!("empty_buffer_80x24", actual);
}
\`\`\`

## Files to Create
- tests/common/golden.rs - comparison utilities

## Acceptance Criteria
- Load/save golden files
- Diff detection works
- HTML diff for CI artifacts

 {"id":"bd-1gy","title":"demo_showcase spec: deterministic tour script (auto-demo steps)","description":"# demo_showcase Spec — Deterministic Tour Script

## Why Tour Mode Exists

The showcase must be *demoable without a human driver*.

Tour mode provides:

- a deterministic sequence for recording videos
- a “guided explanation” overlay for first-time users
- an automatic prove-out of advanced features

## Tour Model

- Tour is a sequence of steps: `Step { title, description, duration_ms, action }`
- Each step can:
  - mutate app state (select panel, scroll, open overlay, type text)
  - trigger an animation or highlight effect
  - pop a toast describing what’s happening
- Determinism:
  - fixed durations
  - seeded animation parameters (no randomness; if “random”, derive from step index)

## Proposed Steps (Feature-Proving Sequence)

1. **Welcome + No Flicker**
   - Show title + smooth animated accent bar.
   - Toast: “Diff rendering + synchronized output (if supported).”

2. **Scissor-Clipped Sidebar Scroll**
   - Auto-scroll sidebar list inside clip rect.
   - Visual: scroll indicator.

3. **Focus + Hit Testing**
   - Simulate “clicking” top bar buttons (help/palette) and switching panel focus.
   - Visual: border glow moves.

4. **Command Palette (Glass Overlay)**
   - Open palette overlay.
   - Demonstrate opacity stack + blended background.

5. **Editor: Typing + Undo/Redo**
   - Insert a few lines.
   - Undo/redo quickly.
   - Toast: “Rope + undo groups.”

6. **Editor: Highlighting + Theme Switch**
   - Turn on syntax highlighting.
   - Switch theme (Dark → Light → High contrast).

7. **Bracketed Paste Proof**
   - Inject a synthetic Paste event into editor.
   - Toast: “Bracketed paste parsed and inserted.”

8. **Unicode + Width + Grapheme Pool**
   - Render a line containing:
     - CJK wide chars
     - single-codepoint emoji
     - multi-codepoint ZWJ emoji (must require pool)
   - Show a small “width ruler” under it.

9. **Preview: PixelBuffer + Supersampling**
   - Animate a small pixel-art + sparkline chart.

10. **Alpha Blending / Opacity**
   - Draw a translucent “modal” over preview with a drop-shadow-ish effect.

11. **Hyperlinks (OSC 8)**
   - Render clickable links (docs/GitHub) in the logs panel.
   - Visual: link styling + hover/pressed feedback.

12. **Finale: Performance Overlay**
   - Show render stats (fps, changed cells, bytes estimate).
   - End with a “you can build this” message.

## User Controls

- Start/stop: `Ctrl+T`
- Next: `Enter` / `Space`
- Prev: `Backspace`
- Exit tour: `Esc`

Unattended runs:

- When started with `--exit-after-tour`, the final tour step completion triggers a clean process exit (useful for recordings and PTY E2E).

## Acceptance Style

Tour steps should include micro-copy that explains *why* each feature matters.","acceptance_criteria":"Checklist:
- [ ] Step list covers all advanced features (no gaps)
- [ ] Each step has a deterministic duration + action
- [ ] Tour can run unattended start-to-finish
- [ ] Tour UI explains what’s being demonstrated","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:37.649964906Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:32:17.441750420Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:32:17.441604188Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","design","tour"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1gy","depends_on_id":"bd-1ei","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:07.758580540Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1gy","depends_on_id":"bd-1ok","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:09.880209710Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1gy","depends_on_id":"bd-2jc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:37.672251398Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1gy","depends_on_id":"bd-2mu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:03.007134873Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1gy","depends_on_id":"bd-3l0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:05.596193414Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1hd","title":"Integration Example","description":"## Overview
Create a working example demonstrating the full rendering loop with input handling.

## Scope
Simple text editor example showing:
- Terminal setup/cleanup
- Input event loop
- Rendering pipeline
- Proper error handling
- Debug logging capability

## Implementation
```rust
// examples/simple_editor.rs
use opentui::prelude::*;
use std::io::{stdin, stdout, Read, Write};
use std::env;

fn main() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    // Check for debug mode
    let debug = env::var("OPENTUI_DEBUG").is_ok();
    
    // Setup logging
    if debug {
        eprintln!("[DEBUG] OpenTUI Simple Editor starting");
    }
    
    // Get terminal size
    let (width, height) = terminal_size()?;
    if debug {
        eprintln!("[DEBUG] Terminal size: {}x{}", width, height);
    }
    
    // Setup
    let _guard = enable_raw_mode()?;
    let mut renderer = Renderer::new(width as u32, height as u32)?;
    let mut buffer = EditBuffer::with_text("Hello, OpenTUI!\n\nPress Ctrl+Q to quit.");
    let mut view = EditorView::new(buffer);
    view.set_line_numbers(true);
    view.set_wrap_mode(WrapMode::Word);
    
    if debug {
        eprintln!("[DEBUG] Editor initialized");
    }
    
    // Event loop
    let mut parser = InputParser::new();
    let mut input_buf = [0u8; 64];
    let mut frame_count = 0u64;

    loop {
        frame_count += 1;
        
        // Render
        renderer.clear();
        view.render_to(renderer.buffer_mut(), 0, 0, width as u32, height as u32);
        renderer.present(&mut stdout())?;
        
        if debug && frame_count % 60 == 0 {
            let cursor = view.edit_buffer().cursor();
            eprintln!("[DEBUG] Frame {} - cursor: row={} col={} offset={}",
                frame_count, cursor.row, cursor.col, cursor.offset);
        }

        // Handle input
        let n = stdin().read(&mut input_buf)?;
        
        if debug {
            eprintln!("[DEBUG] Input: {} bytes, hex: {}",
                n, input_buf[..n].iter().map(|b| format!("{:02x}", b)).collect::<Vec<_>>().join(" "));
        }

        for event in parser.parse(&input_buf[..n]) {
            if debug {
                eprintln!("[DEBUG] Event: {:?}", event);
            }
            
            match event {
                InputEvent::Key(KeyEvent { code: KeyCode::Char('q'), modifiers })
                    if modifiers.contains(Modifiers::CTRL) => {
                    if debug {
                        eprintln!("[DEBUG] Ctrl+Q detected, exiting");
                    }
                    return Ok(());
                }
                InputEvent::Key(key) => {
                    view.handle_key(key);
                    view.scroll_to_cursor(width as u32, height as u32);
                }
                InputEvent::Mouse(mouse) => {
                    view.handle_mouse(mouse);
                }
                InputEvent::Resize { width: w, height: h } => {
                    if debug {
                        eprintln!("[DEBUG] Resize to {}x{}", w, h);
                    }
                    renderer.resize(w as u32, h as u32);
                    view.set_viewport(0, 0, w as u32, h as u32);
                }
                _ => {}
            }
        }
    }
}
```

## Files to Create
- examples/simple_editor.rs - basic editor example (~100 lines)
- examples/README.md - example documentation

### README.md Content
```markdown
# OpenTUI Examples

## Simple Editor

A minimal text editor demonstrating the OpenTUI rendering loop.

### Running

```bash
cargo run --example simple_editor
```

### Debug Mode

Enable verbose logging:

```bash
OPENTUI_DEBUG=1 cargo run --example simple_editor 2>debug.log
```

### Controls

- Arrow keys: Move cursor
- Ctrl+Q: Quit
- Ctrl+S: Save (if implemented)
- Page Up/Down: Scroll
- Home/End: Line start/end

### What This Demonstrates

1. **Terminal Setup**: Raw mode, alternate screen
2. **Input Handling**: Parsing ANSI sequences into events
3. **Rendering Loop**: Double-buffered, diff-based updates
4. **Resource Cleanup**: RAII guard restores terminal state
```

## Testing Requirements

### Manual Test Checklist (with logging verification)
Run with OPENTUI_DEBUG=1 and verify:
- [ ] Terminal size logged on startup
- [ ] Input bytes logged for each keypress
- [ ] Events logged (Key, Mouse, Resize)
- [ ] Cursor position logged periodically
- [ ] Ctrl+Q exit logged
- [ ] No panic on resize
- [ ] Terminal restored on exit (even after panic)

### Automated Smoke Test (tests/e2e/example_smoke.rs)
```rust
#[test]
fn test_example_compiles() {
    // Just verify the example compiles
    use std::process::Command;
    
    let output = Command::new("cargo")
        .args(["build", "--example", "simple_editor"])
        .output()
        .expect("Failed to run cargo");
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] Build stdout: {}", String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout));
    eprintln!("[TEST] Build stderr: {}", String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr));
    
    assert!(output.status.success(), "Example should compile");
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] PASS: Example compiles successfully");
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Example compiles and runs
- [ ] Demonstrates full input/output loop
- [ ] Handles Ctrl+Q to quit
- [ ] Properly cleans up terminal on exit
- [ ] Debug mode available via OPENTUI_DEBUG env var
- [ ] Debug logs show input bytes, events, and cursor state
- [ ] README explains how to run and debug
- [ ] No panic on common terminal operations

 {"id":"bd-37p","title":"[EPIC] demo_showcase: advanced features prove-out (graphemes, hitgrid, hyperlinks, pixels)","description":"# Epic: demo_showcase — Advanced Feature Prove‑Out

## Purpose

Ensure the demo app explicitly and recognizably exercises OpenTUI’s “wow” features:

- Grapheme pool rendering (multi-codepoint emoji / ZWJ sequences)
- Hyperlinks via OSC 8
- Hit testing grid + mouse interactions
- Pixel-level rendering primitives (supersampling / grayscale)
- Scissor + opacity stacks in nested UI contexts

## Key Principle

These features must not appear as isolated “hello world” snippets.
They should be integrated into believable UI behaviors (preview panels, clickable controls, tooltips, etc.).","acceptance_criteria":"Checklist:
- [ ] Grapheme pool is exercised with a visible “this would break without pool” string
- [ ] Hyperlink cells are rendered and clickable in supported terminals

- [ ] PixelBuffer/GrayscaleBuffer output is integrated into the UI, not a separate screen

 {"id":"bd-383","title":"[EPIC] demo_showcase: tests, docs, and quality gates","description":"# Epic: demo_showcase — Tests, Docs, Quality Gates

## Purpose

A flagship demo that breaks silently is worse than no demo.

This epic ensures we have layered confidence:

- fast compile coverage
- deterministic headless runtime checks
- snapshot regressions for UI/feature markers
- optional true E2E PTY tests for ANSI/terminal behavior
- scripts to run and collect artifacts
- documentation so the demo is discoverable

## Deliverables (This Epic Owns)

### CI-Friendly

- Compile coverage for the new binary target
- Headless smoke mode + test that runs it
- Snapshot regression suite (insta) based on headless JSON output
- Tour determinism regression suite (headless tour + JSON)

### Local / Developer Workflows

- Optional PTY-based E2E tests (ignored by default) that assert on ANSI sequences
- One-command scripts in `scripts/` to run checks and collect artifacts

### Documentation

- README: how to run, flags, tour mode, headless smoke
- In-app help overlay is the primary keybind reference

## Constraints

- Tests must be deterministic and CI-friendly.
- Avoid fragile “golden screenshot” tests that depend on terminal quirks.
- When tests fail, logs must be actionable (include reproduction commands + stored artifacts).","acceptance_criteria":"Checklist:
- [ ] `cargo test` includes a compile test for `demo_showcase`
- [ ] Headless smoke tests validate core layout/render functions
- [ ] README includes how to run the demo + what it proves
- [ ] In-app help overlay documents keybinds and features
- [ ] `cargo fmt --check` + `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` stay clean","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:04:51.321133317Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T05:18:07.067922913Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T05:18:07.067865857Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","docs","epic","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-383","depends_on_id":"bd-1co","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:04:51.345376964Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-397z","title":"E2E: Structured Logging Harness","description":"# E2E Structured Logging Harness (Consolidated)

Consolidated from bd-397z, bd-2pfc, and bd-2vka.

## Components

### 1. LogCapture Struct
- Captures stdout/stderr with timestamps
- Thread-safe for parallel tests
- Configurable buffer size

### 2. Structured JSONL Format
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-01-28T12:00:00.000Z",
  "test_name": "test_tour_mode_lifecycle",
  "event_type": "test_start|step|assertion|error|test_end",
  "level": "trace|debug|info|warn|error|fatal",
  "phase": "setup|execute|verify|teardown",
  "duration_ms": 42,
  "sequence_num": 1,
  "context": { ... },
  "message": "Human readable message"
}
```

### 3. Context Fields by Event Type

#### test_start
- terminal_size, capabilities, config

#### step
- step_name, input_sent, expected_output

#### assertion  
- assertion_type, expected, actual, passed

#### error
- error_type, message, backtrace, recovery_action

#### test_end
- duration_ms, assertions_passed, assertions_failed

### 4. Ergonomic Macros
```rust
e2e_log!(info, "Sending keystroke", { "key": "Enter" });
e2e_assert!(buffer.get(0,0).unwrap().char() == 'X', "Cell content");
```

### 5. Buffer State Dumps
- Capture buffer at key points
- ANSI-formatted text for visual debugging
- Store as test artifacts

### 6. Artifact Output
- Write to HARNESS_ARTIFACTS_DIR
- Filename includes test name and timestamp
- Compress if > 1MB
- CI artifact capture support

### 7. CI Integration
- Upload logs as artifacts
- Parse for summary statistics
- Fail fast on error-level logs
- Queryable with jq

## Files to Create/Modify
- tests/common/logging.rs - StructuredLogger implementation
- tests/common/mod.rs - add logging macros
- All E2E test files - use new logging

## Acceptance Criteria
- All E2E tests use structured logging
- Logs queryable with `jq`
- CI captures logs as artifacts
- Debug workflow documented
- No ANSI codes in log files (CI-friendly)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"VioletDog","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:48:10.082619527Z","created_by":"claude","updated_at":"2026-01-28T20:30:17.245567059Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T20:30:17.245432178Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-397z","depends_on_id":"bd-4cr6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:48:10.096174268Z","created_by":"claude"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3aps","title":"Unit Tests: Buffer Opacity Stack (currently 4 tests)","description":"# Buffer Opacity Stack Unit Tests

## Current State
- src/buffer/opacity.rs has only 4 tests
- Opacity stack is critical for layered transparency

## What Needs Testing

### Basic Stack Operations
- push() adds to stack
- pop() removes from stack
- current() returns effective opacity
- Stack starts at 1.0 (fully opaque)

### Multiplicative Behavior
- Two 0.5 opacities = 0.25 effective
- Push order doesn't affect final value
- Pop restores previous effective opacity

### Edge Cases
- push(0.0) - fully transparent
- push(1.0) - no change
- push(negative) - should clamp to 0.0
- push(>1.0) - should clamp to 1.0
- pop() on empty stack behavior
- Very deep stack (100+ levels)

### Precision
- Floating point precision with many multiplications
- Known precision issues documented

### Integration
- Opacity applied correctly to cell blending
- blend_with_opacity() behavior

## Files to Modify
- src/buffer/opacity.rs - expand tests module

## Acceptance Criteria
- 12+ new tests for opacity stack
- All edge cases covered
- Precision behavior documented","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:45:02.200042810Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:45:02.214271045Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3aps","depends_on_id":"bd-1rfq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:45:02.214231972Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3cj0","title":"demo_showcase scripts: e2e runner + artifact collection (one command)","description":"# demo_showcase Scripts — E2E Runner + Artifact Collection (One Command)

## Purpose

We want a single, repeatable local workflow to validate the demo end-to-end and collect artifacts for debugging.

This bead adds one or more scripts in `scripts/` that:

- run headless smoke + JSON dump
- run snapshot tests (including degradation matrix)
- optionally run ignored PTY tests
- collect stdout/stderr and captured ANSI bytes into an artifacts directory

## Proposed Script(s)

### 1) scripts/demo_showcase_verify.sh

A fast, CI-like validation pass:

- cargo fmt --check
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
- cargo test

Then (optional but useful for quick manual inspection):

- cargo run --all-features --bin demo_showcase -- --headless-smoke --headless-dump-json --max-frames 10

### 2) scripts/demo_showcase_e2e_pty.sh

Runs ignored PTY tests locally with artifacts enabled:

- HARNESS_ARTIFACTS=1 HARNESS_ARTIFACTS_DIR=target/test-artifacts \
    cargo test --test demo_showcase_pty_e2e -- --ignored --nocapture

## Artifact Conventions

Reuse the existing harness conventions:

- HARNESS_ARTIFACTS=1
- HARNESS_ARTIFACTS_DIR=target/test-artifacts

Artifacts should include:

- headless JSON dumps
- snapshot mismatch output (if any)
- degradation matrix JSON snapshots (bd-2bnv)
- PTY captured ANSI bytes (raw + escaped/hex) when PTY tests run

## Acceptance Notes

- Running the verify script gives a clear PASS/FAIL summary.
- On failure, it prints reproduction commands and points to artifact paths.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when:
- `scripts/demo_showcase_verify.sh` runs fmt + clippy + tests and returns non-zero on failure.
- `scripts/demo_showcase_e2e_pty.sh` runs ignored PTY tests with artifacts enabled and stores outputs under `target/test-artifacts`.
- Scripts print clear PASS/FAIL summaries and reproduction commands on failure.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:38.884053450Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:58:19.265674972Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:58:19.265605041Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","scripts","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3cj0","depends_on_id":"bd-2649","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:59.009326578Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3cj0","depends_on_id":"bd-383","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:38.899710558Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3cj0","depends_on_id":"bd-61g0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:59.644070379Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3gb","title":"Fix integer overflow and unbounded paste buffer vulnerabilities","description":"Fixed three security/reliability issues found during code review: (1) Integer overflow in OptimizedBuffer::new() and resize() using saturating_mul, (2) Integer overflow in HitGrid::new() and resize() using saturating_mul, (3) Unbounded paste_buffer growth in InputParser limited to 10MB max","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-27T16:38:12.288682606Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T16:38:25.436300159Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T16:38:25.436240579Z","close_reason":"Fixed in this session - used saturating_mul for integer overflow prevention and added 10MB limit on paste buffer","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
